recommendation: A restrained, on-brand minimal landing that reads as considered rather than generated — warm paper background, one muted clay accent, an editorial serif headline, and a single plain-spoken CTA, with contrast, visible focus, 48px target, and reduced-motion all handled. It holds at mobile, tablet, and desktop. The five async states are not applicable to a static navigational CTA that triggers no request. Two optional fidelity notes: the button uses a plain ease curve rather than the brand's custom ease-out, and the styles re-declare a subset of brand tokens locally instead of consuming the shared contract (values match today, but a future token edit would not repaint this screen). Wire the CTA's destination before this ships as a real entry point.
assessed: MoatProof.jsx + moatproof.css + main.jsx, inherited Marginalia brand spec + tokens.json, and a rendered snapshot read directly.
